prompt: Do not use <think> tags.
Do not reason before generating.
Your response must begin with <nn> immediately.
Your first output character must be '<'.
Do not write any text before <nn>.
Do not write any text after </tr>.
Output only these three XML blocks in order: <nn>...</nn><hp>...</hp><tr>...</tr>.
You are generating a LEMUR-compatible neural network for dataset='{dataset}', task='{task}', metric='{metric}', epoch={epoch}.
Target accuracy above {acc_4}.
References A, B, C and D are architecturally very different — synthesize a new architecture combining their best ideas.

Always follow this exact code structure: imports, TorchVision class, adaptive_pool_flatten, supported_hyperparameters(), class Net containing __init__, infer_dimensions_dynamically, forward, train_setup, learn.
Never place train_setup or learn outside the Net class.
Never use relative imports — use absolute imports only.
self.device = device must be set at the start of Net.__init__.
Call self.to(self.device) in Net.__init__ BEFORE calling infer_dimensions_dynamically().
In infer_dimensions_dynamically(), define dummy first then use it: dummy = torch.zeros(1, C, 224, 224).to(self.device)
Never reference 'dummy' before defining it. Never use 'x' inside infer_dimensions_dynamically.
Never call x.permute() in forward() — CIFAR-10 input is already in [B,C,H,W] format.
Use at most two backbone models — only from: 'resnet50', 'densenet169', 'mobilenet_v3_large', 'efficientnet_b0', 'efficientnet_b3'.
Always use weights='DEFAULT' when instantiating TorchVision — never 'imagenet' or any other string.
supported_hyperparameters() must return exactly: 'batch', 'dropout', 'epoch', 'lr', 'momentum', 'transform'.
Every key in supported_hyperparameters() must appear at least twice — use prm['key'] not prm.get('key').
The <hp> block must contain a valid JSON dict with exactly these keys: batch, dropout, epoch, lr, momentum, transform.
The 'transform' value in <hp> must be exactly 'norm_256_flip'.
Net.__init__ signature must be: def __init__(self, in_shape, out_shape, prm, device).
self.features() must receive raw input x — never backbone output.
